meta.description: Date period with a start and end datetime

Represents a date period extracted from unstructured text, with a date start and end, optional timezone, the original unstructured mention e.g. 'durante la segunda semana de enero', and the precision confidence period in days (the amount of uncertainty we have given the unstructured mention). If only an approximate period is mentioned (e.g. 'next week', or 'in March'), estimate a period in days in which, from the start date, the actual event date is likely to fall (e.g. 'la semana pasada' -> precision_days=7).

mention.description: El texto tal cual se menciona en la nota. The original text mention of the date range, exactly as it appears in the source.

precision_days.description: When the mention is not an exact date, a reasonable range in days within which the real date should fall. If only an approximate period is mentioned (e.g. 'next week', or 'in March'), estimate a period in days in which, from the start date, the actual date is likely to fall (e.g. 'la proxima semana' -> precision_days=7). Null when the date is exact.

meta.description: Represents a single date extracted from unstructured text, with the parsed datetime, the original unstructured mention, and the precision confidence period in days

date.description: The parsed date in YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS format

mention.description: El texto tal cual se menciona en la nota. The original text mention of the date, exactly as it appears in the source.

precision_days.description: When the mention is not an exact date, a reasonable range in days within which the real date should fall. If only an approximate period is mentioned (e.g. 'next week', or 'in March'), estimate a period in days in which, from the start date, the actual date is likely to fall (e.g. 'la proxima semana' -> precision_days=7). Null when the date is exact.

meta.description: Structured location extracted from text, with hierarchical geographic fields and an optional named place. Each field should only be filled if explicitly mentioned in the source text. The fields neighborhood / zone / place_name are MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE — every named area or place must be classified into exactly one of them. Any residential area with a proper name (colonia, fraccionamiento, named district like 'Centro Historico' or 'Polanco') goes in `neighborhood`. A generic directional or functional zone without a residential proper name (e.g. 'zona norte', 'zona industrial') goes in `zone`. Only a point-like landmark (a single building, venue, monument, plaza, station, intersection) goes in `place_name`. Never duplicate a name across these fields and never put a residential area in `place_name`.

neighborhood.description: Named colonia, fraccionamiento, barrio, unidad habitacional, or any named residential district — a residential area identified by a proper name. Examples: 'Centro', 'Centro Historico', 'El Campanario', 'Polanco', 'Jurica', 'Juriquilla', 'Roma Norte', 'Lomas de Chapultepec', 'Unidad Habitacional Vicente Guerrero'. If the article names a colonia, fraccionamiento or named district, put the name HERE — not in `place_name` and not in `zone`.

zone.description: Generic directional or functional area of a city without a residential proper name. Examples: 'zona norte', 'zona sur', 'zona poniente', 'zona industrial', 'zona metropolitana', 'distrito financiero'. Do NOT use this for named residential districts (those go in `neighborhood`).

place_name.description: Proper name of a single point-like place that occupies one discrete location on the map: a venue (stadium, theater, arena, museum, hospital, school with a proper name), a landmark (monument, plaza, park, fountain, bridge), a transit point (station, terminal, airport, toll booth), a single named building or business (mall, market, hotel, named factory or store), or a named intersection/glorieta. Examples that DO belong here: 'Estadio Corregidora', 'Plaza de Armas', 'Parque Queretaro 2000', 'Aeropuerto Internacional', 'Hospital General de Leon'. Examples that do NOT belong here (each goes elsewhere): 'El Campanario' -> neighborhood, 'Centro Historico' -> neighborhood, 'Polanco' -> neighborhood, 'zona norte' -> zone, 'zona industrial' -> zone, 'frente a la escuela' / 'cerca del mercado' -> null (generic).

meta.description: A price or cost range extracted from text, with the original mention, numeric lower and upper bounds, and currency

mention.description: El texto tal cual se menciona en la nota. Original text mention of the price, e.g. 'precios desde 500 hasta 2500 pesos'

lower.description: Lower bound of the price range (0 if free)

upper.description: Upper bound of the price range (same as lower if single price)

meta.description: Estimated attendance or number of participants extracted from text, with the original mention and a numeric estimate

mention.description: El texto tal cual se menciona en la nota. Original text mention, e.g. 'mas de 5 mil personas'

meta.description: Capacity of a venue or place extracted from text, with the original mention and a numeric value

mention.description: El texto tal cual se menciona en la nota. Original text mention, e.g. 'capacidad para 87 mil personas'

meta.description: Count of casualties or affected people in an incident, with the original mention and numeric estimates for dead, injured, and missing

mention.description: El texto tal cual se menciona en la nota. Original text mention, e.g. '3 muertos y 5 heridos'

dead.description: Number of deaths, 0 if none mentioned

injured.description: Number of injured, 0 if none mentioned

missing.description: Number of missing, 0 if none mentioned

meta.description: A numeric count extracted from text, with the original mention, a best-estimate parsed value, and a confidence range for inexact mentions

mention.description: El texto tal cual se menciona en la nota. Original text mention, e.g. 'tres personas', 'al menos 5 vehiculos', 'decenas de automoviles'

count.description: Best numeric estimate. When the mention is not exact (e.g. 'varios', 'decenas'), provide a reasonable estimate within the confidence range

confidence_range.description: When the mention is not an exact number, a reasonable +/- range within which the real count should fall. E.g. mention='decenas de vehiculos', count=30, confidence_range=20 means the real number is likely between 10 and 50. Null when the count is exact.

meta.description: Reference to a person mentioned in the text, with name and role/description

name.description: Full name of the person if mentioned

organization.description: Organization the person belongs to, if mentioned
